units.entropy: integer output from injected RetryEntropy.nextLong(exclusiveUpperBound), where 0 <= output < exclusiveUpperBound

boundaries.deadline: exclusive: work may start only when monotonicNowMs < operationDeadlineMs
boundaries.commitment: the first request-body octet accepted by the transport changes the operation floor to POSSIBLY_SENT
boundaries.jitter: uniform integer in the inclusive interval [0, capMs]
boundaries.ratio: decompressedBytes <= compressedBytes * expansionRatioLimit; zero compressed bytes may produce only zero decompressed bytes

constants.endpointAppend: strip trailing slashes, then append /v1/messages unless the path already ends exactly in /v1/messages
constants.credentialValidation: every selected fixed, provider, or environment credential must be 1..4096 printable ASCII octets 0x20..0x7e, contain at least one non-SP octet, and preserve leading/trailing SP byte-exactly; absence falls through precedence, but empty, all-SP, controls, DEL, and non-ASCII are terminal CONFIGURATION
constants.baseUrlValidation: validate the raw supplied bytes with a custom lexical parser before platform URI parsing, then platform-parse, serialize, and require a byte-for-byte canonical roundtrip: nonempty absolute lowercase http:// or https:// ASCII RFC3986 only; total raw URI <=2048 bytes, authority <=512 bytes, base path <=1536 bytes; no whitespace, control, backslash, userinfo, query, or fragment; an explicit port is exactly decimal 1..65535 with no sign or leading zero; path permits only slash, unreserved, sub-delims, colon, at-sign, or percent escapes with uppercase hex, and rejects malformed or lowercase escapes, percent-encoded slash or backslash, and dot or dot-dot segments; https accepts a DNS name or canonical IP literal, while http accepts only exact lowercase localhost, canonical four-octet 127.0.0.0/8 without leading zeros, or bracketed ::1; aliases, integer/hex/octal IPv4, expanded or zone-scoped IPv6, mapped IPv6, trailing-dot localhost, and Unicode confusables are rejected; after appending /v1/messages, the origin-form final path without query is <=2048 bytes and the full canonical URI is <=4096 bytes
constants.userAgentValidation: undefined means no suffix; an explicit suffix must be 1..256 printable ASCII octets 0x20..0x7e with no leading or trailing SP; controls including TAB, DEL, non-ASCII, empty, and 257+ octets are construction CONFIGURATION
constants.idempotencyKeyValidation: 1..255 printable ASCII HTTP field-value octets (0x20..0x7e); reject non-ASCII, every ASCII control octet, DEL, and leading or trailing SP before serialization or network; preserve every accepted caller key byte-exact
constants.remoteErrorRule: consume and discard at most 65536 decoded payload bytes under the compressed, decompressed, and expansion caps without JSON decoding or field extraction; retain no remote code, message, request/correlation ID, stack, detail, unknown field, partial field, or unsafe value; byteTrace uses error-discarded and public code/message always come from the SDK errorCatalog
constants.jitterFormula: capMs is the overflow-safe retry-index exponential backoff capped by retryMaxMs and remainingOperationMs; call RetryEntropy.nextLong(exclusiveUpperBound) with exclusiveUpperBound = capMs + 1, require integer output with 0 <= output < exclusiveUpperBound, and use that output as fullJitterMs
constants.retryAfterFormula: parse decimal delta-seconds and future IMF-fixdate with checked signed 64-bit millisecond arithmetic; negative, malformed, past or present, duplicate-conflicting, and overflowing values are invalid and use fullJitterMs; effectiveDelayMs = max(fullJitterMs, min(parsedRetryAfterMs, retryAfterMaxMs, remainingOperationMs)); no work starts when the delay reaches the exclusive operation deadline
constants.acceptedResponse: exactly one accepted JSON Content-Type and a complete identity or single-member gzip JSON object satisfying jsonAmplificationRule; id must match msg_[A-Za-z0-9_-]{1,124}; type, customerId, and strict YYYY-MM-DDTHH:mm:ss.SSSZ timestamp must exactly equal the snapshotted serialized request envelope; unknown fields are ignored only after the same limits and duplicate checks
constants.configurationSnapshot: construction snapshots options, environment, canonical origin, and fixed credential fallbacks once for runtime lifetime; each admitted operation invokes only a configured apiKeyProvider dynamically and snapshots provider result, defaults, idempotency key, and clocks once for all attempts
constants.admission: one nonblocking permit is acquired before credentials, defaults, idempotency, serialization, or network; the same permit is retained across retries and released exactly once
constants.responseContentType: exactly one field/value; trim only HTTP OWS SP/HTAB; require case-insensitive application/json followed by no parameter or exactly one charset parameter with OWS around semicolon and equals and a case-insensitive utf-8 token or unescaped quoted UTF-8; reject duplicate/other parameters, escapes, trailing semicolon, comma-combined values, and application/*+json
constants.jsonPolicy: successful response bodies use fatal UTF-8, no BOM, exactly one JSON value followed only by JSON whitespace, no duplicate object keys, and the exact jsonAmplificationRule; non-2xx bodies are never JSON-decoded
constants.durationValidation: all timeout and backoff durations are positive whole milliseconds in 1..9223372036854; fractional milliseconds, sub-millisecond values, zero, negatives, max+1, and overflow are rejected without truncation using overflow-safe conversion
constants.bufferBudgetRule: one nonblocking per-runtime weighted byte budget covers all retained-or-reserved request/response/parser workspaces, transport reservations, and payload storage; default 67108864 bytes, range 4194304..1073741824; every admitted operation atomically reserves exactly 1048576 REQUEST_SERIALIZATION_WORKSPACE bytes plus 1048576 RESPONSE_WORKSPACE bytes plus a distinct 262144 JSON_PARSER_SCRATCH_RESERVATION, total 2359296 bytes, before observer, configuration, provider, default, idempotency, serialization, custom Transport, DNS, or network side effects; serialization converts request workspace to actual retained request bytes and releases the unused portion while retaining actual request bytes across retries; the response workspace may retain the complete capped raw response while parser scratch independently bounds streaming parser and duplicate structures; response and parser reservations remain across retries until terminal cleanup, with no post-publication byte acquisition; RuntimeDiagnostics.bufferedBytes counts every retained-or-reserved byte; exact atomic permits, no queued waiter, release exactly once; every target overlay must account additional fixed reservations and prove the 4194304 minimum makes progress for one exact-1048576-byte request plus one exact-1048576-byte valid raw and gzip response
constants.admissionLivenessRule: sample monotonic operation start and exclusive deadline synchronously at public admission; caller work is bounded to O(1) clock read plus language-native deadline and ordinary-operation-execution handoff; register independent deadline authority before ordinary execution; every pending execution state-checks before observer, provider, default, key, serialization, and network side effects; cancellation, close, or deadline must produce no late ghost side effect
constants.observerLifecycleRule: observer callbacks are serialized and tracked through a bounded language-native dispatcher even when its delivery resource is borrowed; one delivered callback completes before the next; observer exceptions cannot affect sending; close cancels tracked callbacks cooperatively, never closes borrowed resources, and never waits for its own active callback; close from an observer or send completion may mark CLOSED after initiating shutdown while the current callback exits on return
constants.responseHeaderRule: snapshot response headers once and count each non-pseudo name/list-value pair as UTF8(name)+2+UTF8(value)+2 using overflow-safe arithmetic; exclude HTTP/2 pseudo-headers; maximum 100 field lines and 65536 logical bytes; names are 1..256 ASCII token bytes; values are valid Unicode scalar strings up to 8192 UTF-8 bytes and reject NUL, CR, LF, other C0 controls except HTAB, DEL, and unpaired surrogates; parsed media/security headers are ASCII-only before case folding; no null name/list/value; headers are outside the payload budget
constants.operationIdRule: production operation IDs are generated locally as op_ plus a lowercase RFC4122 UUIDv4 and are not injectable; each runner must assert the actual public error, outcome, and observer IDs match ^op_[0-9a-f]{8}-[0-9a-f]{4}-4[0-9a-f]{3}-[89ab][0-9a-f]{3}-[0-9a-f]{12}$, are identical for one operation, and are unique across operations before normalizing first-seen IDs to op-0001, op-0002, and so on; manifest operation IDs and concurrency operationId values are runner-only aliases, never public values
constants.runtimeDiagnosticsRule: one linearizable immutable runtime-state epoch owns admission, weighted bytes, lifecycle, and every public diagnostic counter behind one language-appropriate synchronization boundary; each causal transition publishes one coherent epoch; public counters saturate exactly at MAX_COUNTER_VALUE=9007199254740991 so JSON and IEEE-754 runtimes preserve them; wider native counters remain private; diagnostics() observes exactly one epoch; closed=true implies inFlightOperations=0 and bufferedBytes=0 in that same epoch
constants.outboundFramingRule: Content-Length is exactly one canonical decimal field equal to actual serialized body bytes 0 or 1..1048576, with no leading zero except the single value 0; never send Transfer-Encoding or chunked framing; H1 is exactly POST origin-form HTTP/1.1 followed by one Host containing the canonical authority with every explicit input port preserved including default ports, one Content-Length, the five outboundRequiredHeaderOrder fields in that order, then zero or one target-authorized traceparent, with no Connection, Expect, Upgrade, TE, duplicate, or extra field; H2 pseudo-fields are exactly :method POST, :scheme, :authority with the canonical authority, :path with the origin-form target in that order, followed by one Content-Length, the five required fields in order, then zero or one target-authorized traceparent, with no regular Host, connection-specific, TE, duplicate, or extra field
constants.runtimeConstructionSnapshotRule: ClientOptions/runtime construction reads options and environment exactly once; resolve precedence explicit baseUrl over present REPOST_API_URL over https://api.repost.sh, validate and snapshot one canonical origin for the runtime lifetime, and snapshot fixed/environment credential fallbacks; an invalid present selected environment base URI is synchronous constructionThrow with snapshot:0 and no operation, outcome, admission, hook, or side effect; later process-environment mutation changes neither route nor credential; only a configured apiKeyProvider is invoked dynamically once per admitted operation
constants.jsonAmplificationRule: successful-response JSON is parsed streaming with maximum open object/array nesting depth 32 including the root container, maximum 10000 parser tokens counting START_OBJECT, END_OBJECT, START_ARRAY, END_ARRAY, FIELD_NAME, and every scalar exactly once, maximum 64 UTF-8 bytes per decoded field name, maximum 8192 Unicode scalar values and 8192 UTF-8 bytes per decoded string scalar, maximum 128 ASCII characters per raw numeric token including sign, decimal point, and exponent, and maximum 16 members per object; exact limits are accepted and the first +1 breach cancels parsing as nonretryable RESPONSE_TOO_LARGE/POSSIBLY_SENT for 2xx; malformed syntax/UTF-8 remains RESPONSE_PROTOCOL; the parser, fixed/prebounded duplicate detection, decoded-name/string windows, and numeric token window use only the distinct pre-admission 262144-byte JSON_PARSER_SCRATCH_RESERVATION, retained across retries and released once at terminal cleanup, with no hidden or post-publication byte acquisition

SERVER_FAILURE.message: Server failed to process the request

executionModeSemantics.core-attempt-seam: Run the generated SDK core with the deterministic one-attempt seam; preserve logical URI, configuration, clock, and entropy assertions without opening a socket.
executionModeSemantics.commitment-probe-seam: Run the unmodified production request-body commitment tracker against an instrumented body publisher; inject one transport failure before client invocation or publisher subscription, open no socket, and prove zero publisher subscriptions and zero demand without a runner-owned commitment state machine.
executionModeSemantics.production-http-fixture: Resolve $fixtureBaseUrl from the fixture handshake and run the unmodified default production HTTP transport against that explicit loopback URL, including private raw-socket request assertions; custom transports and dial rewriting are forbidden.
executionModeSemantics.enterprise-network-fixture: Resolve $networkFixtureUrl from the capability-selected enterprise fixture handshake endpoint and run the actual raw-streaming production transport; exact fixture state, operation count, core-attempt count, and one-shot wire-body publication evidence are mandatory.
executionModeSemantics.custom-one-attempt: Invoke the public custom one-attempt transport seam once per started attempt with distinct effective connectTimeoutMs and attemptTimeoutMs while the core retains admission, operation deadline, retry, serialization, response validation, error, and observer ownership.

hold.semantics: Register one named barrier for the attempt and block when the phase is consumed; releaseAt is an exact byte threshold and is legal only for afterRequestBytes or responseBody.

release.semantics: Release exactly one previously reached and still-blocked barrier; release-before-hold, missing, or duplicate release is a fixture failure.

advanceTime.semantics: Advance only the injected monotonic clock synchronously by byMs; never sleep.

advanceWallTime.semantics: Advance only the injected wall clock synchronously by byMs.

cancelSend.semantics: Cancel the operation when the declared phase or matching hold is reached.

closeClient.semantics: Close the client when the declared phase or matching hold is reached and terminate the in-flight operation.

acceptRequest.semantics: Consume the attempt request and assert only the closed requestAssertion enum without retaining request metadata; a later terminal action supplies its outcome.

captureRequestMetadata.semantics: Register private capture for the attempt, retain exactly captureAllowlist through terminal response progress, assert every captureDenylist key is absent, and emit capture-private:clean only for that exact shape.

disconnect.semantics: Consume the attempt with a disconnect before any request octet or immediately after the first request octet.

failConnection.semantics: Consume the attempt with the declared synthetic connection failure at the declared commitment phase; optional cause message remains private.

respond.semantics: Return one valid factory-constructed response; custom-one-attempt cases may inject a close failure or mutate the caller-owned source header map/list after TransportResponse.of returns to prove defensive snapshot and close-once ownership. The transferred body stream is never caller-mutated or closed. Invalid response objects are forbidden and unconstructible through the supported final/factory-only ABI.

respondChunks.semantics: Consume the attempt and emit chunks in order; omitted complete means true, while false closes the upstream response after the last chunk without a complete body.

respondEchoSentinels.semantics: Consume the attempt and emit the deterministic private sentinel-echo JSON recipe without retaining selected values.

observerFail.semantics: Inject an observer exception at the named closed observer event without changing the send result.

failProxyAuthentication.semantics: Return a proxy-originated 407 before any origin request bytes; map PROXY/NOT_SENT and retain no origin error fields.

drainRuntimeDiagnostics.semantics: Harness-only quiescence point after primary and outcome are observable and private completion/cleanup returned; drain or acknowledge the deterministic observer-delivery queue without sleep, then read exactly one public RuntimeDiagnostics snapshot. Production completion never waits for telemetry.

observerDrop.semantics: Before runtime construction, install a deterministic borrowed observer-delivery seam that rejects the named event offer. Prove its callback is never invoked, increment droppedObserverEvents exactly once, leave observerFailures unchanged, and never change primary/outcome completion.

proveNetworkCapability.semantics: Reset then perform every ordered setup action before client construction/operation start, consume one advertised enterprise capability through the actual production raw transport, and assert every normalized per-operation public outcome plus exact operation/core-attempt/wire-publication counts. expectedState is one or more unique semicolon-delimited state.path=decimal or state.path=limits.handshakeField equality predicates evaluated against the post-operation safe fixture state and handshake limits.

mutateEnvironmentAfterConstruction.semantics: After runtime construction and before send admission, set REPOST_API_URL to http://localhost:4323/mutated and REPOST_SEND_API_KEY to mutated-env-key; the operation must still use the construction-snapshotted canonical route and credential without rereading process environment.

authorization-octets-preserved.construction: UTF-8 bytes of ASCII `Bearer ` concatenated byte-exact with the snapshotted resolved credential
authorization-octets-preserved.comparison: streaming SHA-256 digest of the raw socket field-value octets, byte-exact with no parser trimming, normalization, or rewriting
authorization-octets-preserved.retention: discard raw octets and digests immediately after private comparison; never expose them in fixture state, errors, logs, or public traces

request-shape-headers.requestLine: exactly POST /v1/messages HTTP/1.1 with no query or redirect follow
request-shape-headers.headerOrder: exactly Host, Content-Length, Authorization, Content-Type, Accept-Encoding, User-Agent, Idempotency-Key, then optional traceparent; no other or duplicate field
request-shape-headers.host: exactly one canonical fixture authority with its explicit port preserved
request-shape-headers.contentLength: exactly one canonical decimal field equal to actual request-body bytes, with no leading zero except 0
request-shape-headers.contentType: exactly one Content-Type field with value application/json
request-shape-headers.acceptEncoding: exactly one Accept-Encoding field with value gzip
request-shape-headers.userAgent: exactly one User-Agent field matching ^repost-[a-z0-9.-]+/[0-9A-Za-z.+-]+ contract-suite/1$
request-shape-headers.idempotencyKey: exactly one Idempotency-Key field matching the snapshotted operation key
request-shape-headers.optionalTraceparent: zero or one exact lowercase 55-byte W3C traceparent when target-authorized
request-shape-headers.forbidden: Transfer-Encoding, Connection, Expect, Upgrade, TE, chunked framing, duplicate Host or Content-Length, and every unlisted field
request-shape-headers.retention: inspect raw request line, header names/values/order, and byte count only while serving the request; retain no header value, credential, trace identifier, or body in fixture state, errors, logs, or public traces

user-agent-suffix-boundary-256.construction: SDK-owned runtime identity, one SP, then exactly 256 lowercase u octets
user-agent-suffix-boundary-256.retention: validate and immediately discard the User-Agent value; never expose it in fixture state, errors, logs, or public traces

testTokenSemantics.$bytes: serializedRequestBytes replaces the serializer output with exactly that many deterministic octets after all validation/default side effects
testTokenSemantics.$invalidValue: forces the named validation failure before serialization
testTokenSemantics.$int64Max: decodes to the maximum signed 64-bit integer for overflow checks
testTokenSemantics.$providerError: the private injected provider throws using the declared synthetic cause discriminator and sentinel message; the public contract retains only the mapped closed causeCategory and never a class name, discriminator, or provider message
testTokenSemantics.$fixtureBaseUrl: resolve only for production-http-fixture cases from the one-line loopback fixture handshake; never expose it as public SDK configuration
testTokenSemantics.$asciiBytes: materialize exactly length copies of one printable ASCII byte without embedding boundary-size secrets in the canonical JSON
testTokenSemantics.$networkFixtureUrl: resolve only for enterprise-network-fixture cases from the proveNetworkCapability action's endpointField in the one-line enterprise fixture handshake; never expose it as public SDK configuration
testTokenSemantics.$unpairedSurrogateHeaderName: only as a response-action header key, materialize after JSON parsing to x-repost followed by one unpaired UTF-16 high surrogate U+D800; canonical JSON itself contains scalar Unicode only
testTokenSemantics.$unpairedSurrogateHeaderValue: only as a response-action header value, materialize after JSON parsing to value followed by one unpaired UTF-16 high surrogate U+D800; canonical JSON itself contains scalar Unicode only
testTokenSemantics.$rawMapFixture: materialize only in the four raw-map canonicalization vectors: validate all keys before entry traversal, then snapshot valid entries in unsigned UTF-8 byte lexicographic key order without normalization; the token and raw keys never enter a public SDK surface
testTokenSemantics.$issueFixture: materialize only in the three bounded issue-reporting vectors as a fixed invalid descriptor/model traversal; retain the declared static paths and counts without materializing customer values, raw keys, classes, or exception text

bodyKindsSemantics.base64: decode RFC 4648 base64 to raw octets
bodyKindsSemantics.repeat: emit length copies of the single octet byte
bodyKindsSemantics.paddedAsset: emit the referenced base64 asset followed by ASCII space octets until totalLength; totalLength smaller than the asset is a fixture failure
bodyKindsSemantics.gzipStored: emit RFC 1952 with fixed header 1f8b08000000000000ff, DEFLATE stored blocks of at most 65535 octets, little-endian CRC32 and ISIZE, and no optional fields
bodyKindsSemantics.concatenated: concatenate member bodies byte-for-byte; decoders must reject more than one gzip member
bodyKindsSemantics.jsonRecipe: deterministically materialize compact nested-array or flat-object JSON from shape/count without storing an expanded canonical fixture literal

fixtureProtocol.executionRule: load the complete case and declaratively register all fixture actions and barriers before automatically starting send at logical monotonic time 0 (the injected clock origin); consume actions in script order; only runner actions advance clocks, release barriers, cancel sends, or close the client
fixtureProtocol.barrierRule: hold blocks synchronously when its phase and optional byte threshold are consumed; release names exactly one earlier registered blocked barrier; release-before-hold, missing release targets, and duplicate release are fixture failures; every hold ends by matching release or terminal timeout, cancellation, or close
fixtureProtocol.partialRequestRule: afterRequestBytes releaseAt is the exact number of request-body octets accepted before blocking and must not exceed the deterministic serialized request length; commitment occurs at the first accepted octet
fixtureProtocol.partialResponseRule: responseBody releaseAt is the exact number of response-body octets emitted before blocking and must not exceed the declared response recipe; respondChunks complete defaults to true and false closes upstream after the final declared chunk without completing the body
fixtureProtocol.captureRule: captureRequestMetadata is private harness evidence only; capture-private:clean means the capture has exactly captureAllowlist, every captureDenylist key and value is absent, and the token never appears on a public redaction surface
fixtureProtocol.disconnectRule: beforeRequestBytes disconnects before any request-body octet; afterRequestBytes disconnects immediately after exactly the first request-body octet
fixtureProtocol.orderedResponseRule: all attempt references are nondecreasing, terminal response/disconnect/failure attempts are strictly increasing, and each terminal action is consumed once
fixtureProtocol.echoRule: respondEchoSentinels emits UTF-8 application/json with no whitespace as {"code":"remote","message":sentinels.remoteMessage,"requestId":"req_echo","echo":{...}}; echo keys follow the action sentinels array order and each value is the matching top-level sentinel; the fixture does not store the body or selected values

networkFixture.certificationRule: Every supported production language transport lane must consume the handshake and prove all applicable capabilities with no silent skips; DNS and refused-connect remain platform lanes because the fixture is IPv4-only.
networkFixture.directRule: SDK-created transports are DIRECT and must record zero direct/no-proxy trap hits despite ambient host-runtime and process proxy configuration; only explicit injected transports may proxy.
networkFixture.protocolRule: Prefer negotiated HTTP/2 with HTTP/1.1 fallback; certify multiplexing, per-stream cancellation/reset, GOAWAY metadata and delivery/retry handling, proxy/ALPN redaction, and fallback behavior.

TYPESCRIPT.operation: one ordinary native Promise decorated with non-enumerable non-writable non-configurable outcome and cancel properties; not a Promise subclass
TYPESCRIPT.basicSend: generated event method returns SendOperation and remains await/then compatible
TYPESCRIPT.cancel: SendOperation.cancel() or the caller AbortSignal enters the same idempotent operation cancellation path
TYPESCRIPT.primaryCancellation: reject with the original Node DOMException identity whose name is AbortError and which remains instanceof DOMException
TYPESCRIPT.outcome: the stable read-only outcome Promise always fulfills exactly once with reconciliation metadata
TYPESCRIPT.derived: then/catch/finally return plain native promises whose cancellation or handling cannot mutate the send operation

GO.operation: BeginSend returns *SendOperation with Done, Wait, Outcome, and Cancel; generated BeginX pairs with existing synchronous X
GO.basicSend: Send is exactly BeginSend(...).Wait() and Wait is repeatable
GO.cancel: SendOperation.Cancel() is idempotent and cancels the native operation context
GO.primaryCancellation: return enriched *PublishError whose Unwrap preserves the exact context.Canceled or context.DeadlineExceeded winner for errors.Is
GO.outcome: Outcome waits for terminal state and returns one immutable SendOutcome value with reconciliation metadata
GO.derived: Done closes exactly once and no caller-visible channel or error mutation can settle the operation

PYTHON.runtime: CPython 3.10 through 3.14 with distinct sync RepostClient and async AsyncRepostClient
PYTHON.operation: async begin_send returns an Awaitable SendOperation with cancel, cancelled, outcome, and __await__; generated async begin_x pairs with awaitable x
PYTHON.basicSend: sync send remains synchronous; async send awaits begin_send without mixing sync and async ownership
PYTHON.cancel: SendOperation.cancel() or task cancellation enters the same idempotent operation cancellation path
PYTHON.outcome: operation.outcome remains awaitable under asyncio.shield and returns reconciliation metadata exactly once
PYTHON.derived: cancelling or handling a derived task cannot forge settlement or detach queued retry/network cancellation

issueReporting.validationTraversal: descriptor declaration order, then immutable collection/map snapshot order, depth first and preorder; at most one issue per visited location by ValidationIssueCode declaration priority
issueReporting.configurationOrdering: first ClientOptionKey declaration position, then ConfigurationIssueCode declaration position; CONFLICT and RESOURCE_MISMATCH have exactly two distinct declaration-ordered keys and every other code exactly one
issueReporting.countRule: continue the already bounded traversal after retention fills; issueCount is the total encountered count saturated at maxIssueCount and issuesTruncated is exactly issueCount != retained list size
issueReporting.redactionRule: retain only closed issue enums, static descriptor member paths, fixed list indices, fixed raw-map placeholders, and closed option keys; never retain a value, raw map key, rejected class/type name, iterator text, exception text, or framework config source
issueReporting.rawMapKeyRule: Validate every raw-JSON map key before entry traversal; any non-string key yields exactly one INVALID_JSON issue and any invalid-Unicode key yields exactly one INVALID_UNICODE issue at the container path plus [{*}], with no entry traversal.
issueReporting.rawMapOrderRule: When every key is a Unicode scalar string, compare its unnormalized UTF-8 bytes as unsigned octets lexicographically and use that one snapshot order for validation issues and compact serialization in every target; insertion, shuffle, and host-map iteration order are irrelevant.

rawMapCanonicalization.validKeyOrderRule: unsigned UTF-8 byte lexicographic order, no Unicode normalization

issueFixtures.retentionOrder: first encountered issues whose individual and aggregate static paths fit, up to 32; continue bounded traversal only to count

traceVocabulary.schedule: retry:<positive integer>:<field>=<value>@<monotonic ms>; cap is a nonnegative integer, bound is a positive integer, entropy is an integer, and multiple facts are separate ordered entries

concurrencyProtocol.runtimeRule: Each scenario constructs exactly one production runtime; operationId values are runner-only synchronization aliases while actual public IDs are asserted and normalized under constants.operationIdRule; every operation invokes the real public send pipeline on that same runtime.
concurrencyProtocol.admissionRule: In-flight and weighted payload-byte admission are nonblocking production-runtime linearizations with no waiter; saturation emits OVERLOADED with the causal delivery floor and every rejected or acquired permit is counted exactly.
concurrencyProtocol.completionRule: Admission atomically acquires public in-flight capacity and one finite private settlement reservation. Terminal cleanup releases public in-flight/bytes before language-native primary publication, publishes outcome second, and releases the reservation only after both publication calls return. Target overlays may pin stricter callback-retention and numeric backlog mechanics; direct pre-admission rejects acquire no reservation or fallback settlement.
concurrencyProtocol.synchronizationRule: All fixture scripts and language-neutral execution barriers are registered before start; deterministic checkpoints, manual clock advancement, deadline authority, cancellation, and fallback settlement require no wall-clock sleep and cannot depend on ordinary operation execution making progress.
concurrencyProtocol.diagnosticsRule: Public RuntimeDiagnostics is one immutable linearizable runtime-state epoch containing exactly inFlightOperations, bufferedBytes, reason-specific overload and failure counters, and closed; language implementations may use an atomic reference, mutex/lock, single-event-loop discipline, or equivalent one-boundary synchronization, while internal permit, byte, worker, timer, dispatch, and ghost-work counters remain separate harness proof.
concurrencyProtocol.manifestRule: caseManifest is the single execution inventory: all single-operation case IDs first, then every concurrency scenario ID; each runner reports one ordered executed-ID list byte-equal to caseManifest with no skip or unsupported outcome.
concurrencyProtocol.legacyOccupiedPermitsRule: occupiedPermits is retained only for the three legacy single-operation vectors and MUST be expanded by a runner into real held sends on the same production runtime; it is deprecated harness syntax and contributes no admission coverage or release-race evidence.

seedDiagnosticCounter.semantics: Before the counter's causal events, use the supported deterministic diagnostics test seam to seed one public monotonic counter at an exact nonnegative value no greater than MAX_COUNTER_VALUE; subsequent real events prove exact saturation without billions of iterations.

start.semantics: Invoke one asynchronous production send and return after its admission decision or first deterministic block/schedule/terminal point.

awaitBarrier.semantics: Wait until the named operation reaches its pre-registered production transport barrier without advancing logical time.

release.semantics: Release exactly one reached and blocked fixture barrier.

awaitRetryScheduled.semantics: Wait until the production retry pipeline has registered the named retry on the injected deterministic scheduler without running it.

advanceTime.semantics: Advance the injected monotonic clock and run every newly due deterministic scheduler task in stable registration order.

cancel.semantics: Cancel the actual public operation control at the declared reached phase and await its terminal settlement.

closeRuntime.semantics: Invoke and await the production runtime close path; it cancels admitted work, drains inFlight to zero, and transitions to CLOSED.

awaitTerminal.semantics: Require that the public operation is already terminal without releasing a barrier or advancing logical time; a queued implementation deadlocks and fails the harness watchdog.

checkpoint.semantics: Read production runtime diagnostics immediately; queuedWaiters is always zero because the runtime has no admission queue.

hold.semantics: Start the declared attempt through the production transport and block before any request byte is accepted until a release, cancellation, or runtime close.

failConnection.semantics: Fail the declared production attempt before any request byte is accepted; dns is the only concurrency-fixture failure kind.

respond.semantics: Complete the production attempt with the declared logical response byte weight and a valid, remoteError, or malformed deterministic body by transferring/reusing bytes within the response workspace reserved atomically at operation admission; no response-side byte acquisition or rejection occurs after publication.

concurrencyProtocol.orderedTraceGrammar: Permit/reservation/observer/barrier/retry/primary/outcome/terminal/close/checkpoint grammar uses runner aliases only; completionRoute is one of OPERATION_EXECUTION, TERMINAL_DISPATCHER, NATIVE_CANCEL, or DIRECT_PRE_ADMISSION.

concurrencyScenarios.description: An immediately accepted operation on a production runtime with maxInFlight one releases its sole permit; a real successful same-runtime probe then reacquires and releases that permit, making any leak observable as OVERLOADED.

A retry that eventually succeeds on a production runtime with maxInFlight one releases its sole permit only after the successful retry; a real successful same-runtime probe then reacquires and releases that permit.

Exactly maxInFlight operations are admitted on one production runtime; the next operation is rejected immediately before every downstream side effect, then a successful same-runtime probe proves released capacity is reusable.
A retrying operation retains one production permit throughout backoff; an overlapping send is rejected and the retry never reacquires or double releases.
Concurrent accepted and HTTP-rejected operations release exactly once; a successful same-runtime probe runs after each terminal path while the other path remains independently observable.
Cancelling a reached production transport barrier releases its permit exactly once, and a later successful send on the same runtime proves that capacity is reusable.
Two deterministic HTTP 500 responses exhaust maxAttempts as SERVER_FAILURE/POSSIBLY_SENT, release before terminal settlement, and permit a later successful send on the same runtime.
Two deterministic malformed HTTP 200 responses exhaust maxAttempts as RESPONSE_PROTOCOL/POSSIBLY_SENT, release before terminal settlement, and permit a later successful send on the same runtime.
Closing one production runtime with two reached sends transitions OPEN to CLOSING to CLOSED, settles both sends, and releases each permit exactly once.
A send started after production runtime close is rejected as CLOSED/NOT_SENT before permit acquisition, observers, configuration, credentials, defaults, idempotency, serialization, or network.
At capacity, overflow is terminal before the held operation is released; awaitTerminal and diagnostics prove there is no admission queue or latent waiter.

One operation atomically reserves the portable 2359296-byte request, response, and parser-scratch set and converts request workspace to an exact retained 1 MiB request. A concurrent operation cannot acquire another complete set from the 4 MiB minimum and fails before observer/configuration/hooks/serialization/network with OVERLOADED/NOT_SENT and no partial byte acquisition. Releasing the held operation and then admitting an exact-1 MiB request plus exact-1 MiB response proves response/parser reservations have no post-publication rejection and all permits are reusable.

Execute this deterministic barrier schedule 1000 times: one atomic 2359296-byte portable request/response/parser acquisition, a second pre-side-effect full-set rejection, terminalization, and close are each sampled from one immutable RuntimeState; counters are monotonic and no snapshot may combine closed=true with nonzero in-flight or buffered bytes.

The deterministic diagnostics seam seeds concurrencyOverloadRejections at MAX_COUNTER_VALUE-1. Two real direct capacity rejections publish MAX_COUNTER_VALUE then remain exactly saturated, after which release and a successful probe prove the counter boundary did not corrupt admission state.

TransportResponse.of defensively snapshots the caller-owned source header map/list; mutating that source after the factory returns cannot alter parsing, while body ownership remains exclusively transferred to the response.

A valid acquired custom 202 response with an over-limit snapshotted header closes exactly once; the first limit breach preserves the status-derived primary classification and increments only responseHeaderLimitFailures.

error.message: Server failed to process the request

legacyBody.message: Server failed to process the request

cases.description: A 409 leaves possible acceptance unresolved, so a later precommit DNS failure cannot lower the operation delivery floor below POSSIBLY_SENT.
A definitive 429 rejection resolves the first attempt, so a later precommit DNS failure remains precisely NOT_SENT rather than inheriting stale uncertainty.
The portable raw URI parser accepts the exact 1536-byte canonical ASCII base-path boundary and appends the fixed endpoint without platform normalization.
A base path above 1536 raw ASCII bytes is rejected before platform URI parsing.
A raw base URI above 2048 total ASCII bytes is rejected before platform URI parsing.
An authority above 512 raw ASCII bytes is rejected before platform URI parsing.
A lowercase percent escape is not canonical and is rejected before platform URI parsing.
A truncated percent escape is rejected before platform URI parsing.
A percent-encoded slash is rejected so path structure cannot change during parsing.
A percent-encoded backslash is rejected so authority/path structure cannot be confused.
A dot path segment is rejected before resolution or normalization.
A dot-dot path segment is rejected before resolution or normalization.
Raw non-ASCII URI input is rejected before platform parsing.
Raw ASCII control input is rejected before platform parsing.
A present selected invalid REPOST_API_URL is synchronously rejected while constructing the single-origin runtime, before any operation, outcome, admission, hook, or side effect.
REPOST_API_URL and REPOST_SEND_API_KEY are snapshotted once at construction; mutating both process variables before send changes neither the canonical endpoint path nor the Authorization credential.
A root object plus 31 nested arrays reaches the exact open-container depth 32 and remains accepted.
The first open-container depth 33 breach is non-retryable RESPONSE_TOO_LARGE/POSSIBLY_SENT.
Exactly 10000 parser tokens, including structure delimiters and field names, are accepted.
The first parser token above 10000 is non-retryable RESPONSE_TOO_LARGE/POSSIBLY_SENT.
An unknown decoded field name at exactly 64 UTF-8 bytes is accepted.
An unknown decoded field name at 65 UTF-8 bytes is rejected before it can be retained.
An unknown ASCII string at exactly 8192 Unicode scalar values and UTF-8 bytes is accepted.
An unknown ASCII string at 8193 scalar values and bytes is rejected.
An unknown 4096-scalar two-byte UTF-8 string at exactly 8192 bytes is accepted.
An unknown 4097-scalar string at exactly 8193 UTF-8 bytes isolates and rejects the byte cap.
An unknown raw numeric token at exactly 128 ASCII characters is accepted without materialization.
An unknown raw numeric token at 129 ASCII characters is rejected without BigInteger allocation.
A response object with exactly 16 members, including the four required fields, is accepted.
A response object member count of 17 is rejected by the fixed duplicate-detection bound.
